macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Markets focus on Fed commentary and Treasury yields as traders await further policy signals
items.summary: Recent market coverage highlights investors watching Federal Reserve commentary, with short- and long-dated Treasury yields remaining a key driver of equity sentiment. Newsflow also points to continued sensitivity to inflation and growth data as markets position for the next policy move.

items.title: US macro calendar packed with housing, consumer, and Fed-related releases
items.summary: The latest US economic calendar includes Redbook sales, pending home sales, housing starts, the Fed balance sheet, and speeches from Fed officials, underscoring a data-heavy session for rates and equities. These releases are likely to influence expectations for growth, inflation, and the timing of any future policy easing.

items.title: Global economic indicators show inflation pressure remains in focus
items.summary: International economic coverage notes persistent inflation concerns across several countries, with central banks continuing to balance price stability against slower growth. Recent headlines also mention macro risks tied to energy prices, geopolitical tensions, and weaker growth in some emerging markets.

items.title: Recent economic analysis points to mixed results in major developed economies
items.summary: Latest commentary from economic research sources highlights mixed signals in housing, inflation, and consumer sentiment, suggesting uneven momentum in developed markets. Central bank communication remains a key theme as policymakers weigh stubborn inflation against signs of cooling demand.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $77,000 as macro pressure and ETF outflows weigh on sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around the $77,000 level amid renewed macro uncertainty, with a U.S. credit downgrade adding pressure to risk assets. The move came alongside roughly $648 million in ETF outflows, reinforcing a cautious tone across crypto markets.

items.title: Strategy buys $2 billion more Bitcoin in a single week
items.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy reportedly added another $2 billion worth of Bitcoin over the past week, signaling continued institutional conviction despite price volatility. The purchase stands out as one of the largest recent corporate accumulations of BTC.

items.title: Crypto market sees $159 million in liquidations over 24 hours
items.summary: Over the last day, leveraged crypto positions were heavily flushed out, with both long and short liquidations across major assets. Bitcoin and Ethereum accounted for a large share of the forced unwind as volatility remained elevated.

items.title: SEC reportedly advances major market-structure and IPO overhaul efforts
items.summary: Market observers say the SEC is moving ahead with a broader overhaul affecting market structure and IPO rules, with implications for crypto-linked capital markets. The discussion comes as stablecoin supply continues to expand beyond $300 billion.

items.title: JPMorgan says ETH and altcoins are lagging Bitcoin on weak network activity
items.summary: JPMorgan analysts reportedly argued that Ethereum and other altcoins are underperforming Bitcoin due to softer network fundamentals. The view adds to the relative strength narrative favoring BTC over the broader alt market.
items.source: NewsNow / JPMorgan cited via crypto coverage

items.title: Ripple and Project Eleven collaborate on XRP Ledger quantum-readiness effort
items.summary: The XRP Ledger is being positioned for a quantum-resistant future through a collaboration involving Ripple and Project Eleven. The project underscores growing attention to long-term blockchain security risks.

items.title: Checker raises $8 million to build stablecoin infrastructure
items.summary: Checker secured $8 million from Galaxy Ventures and other investors to expand stablecoin rails and infrastructure. The funding reflects continued VC interest in payment and settlement layers tied to stablecoins.

items.title: Japan’s ruling party backs an AI-blockchain financial system plan
items.summary: Japan’s LDP is supporting a plan that blends AI and blockchain into the country’s financial system, with an eye toward modernization and yen protection. The move highlights continued policy-level experimentation with digital finance in Asia.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Cloud unveils new AI models and agent tools at Google I/O 26
items.summary: Google Cloud announced Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini Omni, expanded Google Antigravity capabilities, Gemini Spark, and an AI Content Detection API for identifying synthetic media. The company says the releases are aimed at making agentic development and AI-powered work more practical across enterprise products.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 draws massive player interest on Steam
items.summary: Reports highlight that Forza Horizon 6 launched strongly, reaching more than 270,000 players on Steam shortly after release. The strong start makes it one of the most notable recent game launches in the racing genre.

items.title: PlayStation reportedly shifts away from day-and-date PC releases for major story-driven exclusives
items.summary: Gaming discussion around the latest industry news suggests Sony is pulling back from releasing some major single-player exclusives on PC at launch. The move would mark a notable change in PlayStation’s PC strategy.

items.title: PlayStation Plus price increases reported in select regions
items.summary: Recent gaming coverage says Sony has raised PlayStation Plus prices for new subscribers in certain regions. The change is drawing attention as part of broader PlayStation business updates.

items.title: Xbox launches Player Voice to gather community feedback
items.summary: Microsoft introduced Player Voice, a new initiative designed to let Xbox players submit and track feedback more directly. The program is being framed as part of Xbox’s effort to appear more responsive to users.

items.title: Subnautica sales success continues to stand out
items.summary: Recent gaming coverage and discussion point to Subnautica’s strong sales performance as a major industry success story. The franchise continues to be cited as a standout example of an enduring hit.

items.title: New Xbox controllers reportedly surface, including Elite Series 3 and a cloud-focused pad
items.summary: A podcast recap mentions leaks of two unannounced Xbox controllers, one being an Elite Series 3 and another described as a Cloud controller. If accurate, the devices would be a significant upcoming accessory reveal.

items.title: Nintendo reportedly preparing a new Direct by mid-June
items.summary: A recent gaming discussion cites a rumor that Nintendo plans to hold a new Direct presentation by mid-June. Such an event could bring new announcements for Switch and Switch 2 software.

items.title: LEGO 2K Drive reportedly being delisted after three years
items.summary: Gaming coverage in recent discussion says LEGO 2K Drive is being removed from sale after only three years. Delistings like this often draw attention because they limit future access to a live game.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung teases its Galaxy Z TriFold, its most advanced foldable yet
items.summary: Samsung is preparing a new dual-hinge foldable that opens into a 10-inch display from a pocketable form factor. The device appears positioned as a major hardware showcase in the foldables market.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to expand Android beyond phones and tablets by scaling it to desktop computers. The effort is aimed at challenging Microsoft and Apple in personal computing.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is extending Alexa+ from devices to the web, broadening access to its assistant and moving further into agent-style workflows. The launch deepens competition with Google and OpenAI in consumer AI products.

items.title: Qualcomm launches new Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 platforms
items.summary: Qualcomm has unveiled updated wearable chip platforms focused on battery life, industrial design, and connectivity. The move targets key constraints that have limited wider adoption of smartwatches and other wearables.

items.title: Quantum Motion deploys first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: The company says it has deployed a quantum computer manufactured using conventional silicon chip processes. If validated, the milestone could improve the path toward scalable quantum hardware.

items.title: Samsung unveils a 6K gaming monitor among new display products
items.summary: Samsung is expanding its monitor lineup with a 6K gaming display and a larger MovingStyle model. The announcement adds to a busy week of consumer hardware launches.

items.title: Apple previews new accessibility features for Vision Pro and iPhone
items.summary: Apple is rolling out accessibility updates that include natural-language voice input and a feature that lets Vision Pro users control a wheelchair with their eyes. The additions highlight a broader focus on accessibility in its software ecosystem.

items.title: Plex is tripling the price of its lifetime subscription
items.summary: Plex says its premium lifetime plan will rise sharply, reaching $750 later this month. The change is one of the most notable consumer software pricing moves in the latest batch of tech news.
